en.title: {{Full-Stack}} Software Developer
en.text: Improved the security, usability, reliability, and testing of NFIS and NFI web systems by updating authentication to [[OWASP]] standards, strengthening validation and [[logging]], expanding automated tests, [[automating performance testing]], and fixing data integrity and server issues

details.title: Access Security Updates
details.text: Enhanced the security of the NFIS login system, updating verification processes to comply with OWASP standards. Added logging throughout the system, created documentation on logging practices, and gave presentations on security tools (Graudit, OWASP Zap). Developed and updated JUnit and Selenium tests.

details.title: Data Request Feature
details.text: Improved usability and functionality of the NFI Data Request platform by fixing bugs, implementing user validation, and ensuring data integrity. Collaborated with admins to gather requirements and iteratively refine features.

details.title: Performance Testing
details.text: Enhanced Apache JMeter tests for server feature validation, writing Groovy scripts for multi-server testing and a Bash script to automate configuration file population, streamlining the testing process and identifying previously undetected bugs.

en.title: {{Full-Stack}} Manufacturing Software Developer
en.text: I was a [[full-stack software developer]] in the Manufacturing Engineering department at Bailey Electronics. In this role, I was responsible for maintaining and updating the Production Dashboard, a web application that uses backend API calls and synchronizes with both a third-party database system and a local database. The dashboard supports numerous production operations across multiple departments, including printing work orders, designing and printing various labels, generating reports, tracking inventory, and reporting/resolving issues. I was also responsible for creating and updating several Grafana dashboards.

details.title: Containers
details.text: I developed the Containers system to support the organization and shipment of high-volume work orders, particularly for the new PLJ line, where existing processes for handling serialized assemblies were no longer robust enough. The tool was created to improve how large quantities of units were grouped, tracked, and prepared for shipment, with a strong focus on usability in a real production environment. By organizing serialized assemblies into manageable container groups, it improved traceability, shipment organization, and issue isolation on high-volume builds. This system successfully supported 21 work orders on the PLJ line totaling 4,000 units.

details.title: Issue Escalation Interface
details.text: I designed and developed the Issue Escalation Interface to improve how issues are reported, communicated, and resolved. Before this system, issues were tracked manually on whiteboards and passed between departments through meetings and informal communication, making it difficult to maintain visibility, assign ownership, and retain information for future reference. This system created a centralized process for reporting issues, routing them to the appropriate people based on role, tracking ownership and resolution progress, and preserving a complete history of each issue. In addition to supporting faster and more organized responses to problems, it also captures data that can be used to identify recurring issues, measure resolution time, and improve decision-making. I led the initiative from concept through implementation and training.

details.title: Part Shortage Report
details.text: This tool was created to address a recurring production problem: work orders were being delayed because required parts were backordered, which led to late shipments, customer frustration, and lost revenue. At the time, the team’s only visibility into these shortages was a tool on the picking terminal that looked just five days ahead, which was not enough time to respond effectively. I developed a tool that generates the same report for any future date, making it possible to identify risks earlier and plan orders more effectively. By giving the company better advance visibility into part shortages, this tool helps reduce delays, improve on-time completion of work orders, and support more reliable delivery to customers.
